# UC-22.14.58 · System Component Inventory vs Observed Network Assets

## Description

Reconciles CMDB-managed systems with active DHCP/DNS or scan observations for CM-8 system component inventory.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes net and sourcetypes infoblox:dhcp. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize hostname case; (2) dedupe NAT; (3) feed discovery to CMDB; (4) monthly rogue asset report. Indexes required: `index=net`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp`; Lookups: `cmdb_hosts`, `cmdb_hosts.csv`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Infoblox (TA) or DHCP logs, CMDB lookup.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `infoblox:dhcp` or `msdns:debug`, `cmdb_hosts.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize hostname case; (2) dedupe NAT; (3) feed discovery to CMDB; (4) monthly rogue asset report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=net sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp earliest=-24h
| stats latest(mac) as mac, latest(fingerprint) as fp by hostname, ip
| lookup cmdb_hosts.csv hostname OUTPUT owner, classification
| where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")
```

Understanding this SPL

**System Component Inventory vs Observed Network Assets (CM-8)** — Reconciles CMDB-managed systems with active DHCP/DNS or scan observations for CM-8 system component inventory.

Documented **Data sources**: `infoblox:dhcp` or `msdns:debug`, `cmdb_hosts.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Infoblox (TA) or DHCP logs, CMDB lookup. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: net; **sourcetype**: infoblox:dhcp. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=net, sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hostname, ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unknown assets), Map (subnet), Single value (count)

## SPL

```spl
index=net sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp earliest=-24h
| stats latest(mac) as mac, latest(fingerprint) as fp by hostname, ip
| lookup cmdb_hosts.csv hostname OUTPUT owner, classification
| where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")
```

## Visualization

Table (unknown assets), Map (subnet), Single value (count). Key fields: `fp`, `hostname`, `ip`, `mac`.
